Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(auth): OAuth 2.1 proxy AS with SDK v1.27 (#5)

* feat(auth): add OAuth 2.1 proxy AS with SDK v1.27 auth router

Replace custom auth middleware with SDK's mcpAuthRouter, which provides
all standard OAuth 2.1 endpoints (AS metadata, authorize, token, register,
revoke) plus RFC 9728 protected resource metadata.

The server now acts as a proxy Authorization Server that delegates user
authentication to the Elnora platform login page and issues its own
MCP access tokens — never passing through the client's token (CoSAI MCP-T1).

Key changes:
- Upgrade @modelcontextprotocol/sdk from ^1.12.0 to ^1.27.0
  (fixes CVE GHSA-345p-7cg4-v4c7)
- Add ElnoraOAuthProvider implementing OAuthServerProvider interface
- Add InMemoryClientsStore for Dynamic Client Registration (RFC 7591)
- PKCE S256 mandatory (handled by SDK)
- Short-lived access tokens (1h) with refresh token rotation (CoSAI MCP-T7)
- Audience binding via RFC 8707 resource parameter (CoSAI MCP-T9)
- Platform OAuth callback route for auth code relay
- New env vars: ELNORA_PUBLIC_URL, ELNORA_LOGIN_URL,
  ELNORA_TOKEN_EXCHANGE_URL, ELNORA_PLATFORM_CLIENT_ID,
  ELNORA_PLATFORM_CLIENT_SECRET

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(security): add CoSAI security hardening (CORS, scopes, logging, rate limits, SBOM)

Implements 5 CoSAI MCP security controls:

1. CORS + CSRF (MCP-T7): Restrictive CORS policy — only allows
   configured origins (MCP server + platform). Blocks cross-origin
   attacks. CSRF on /oauth/callback via mcp_code session validation.

2. Tool invocation logging (MCP-T12): Structured JSON audit log for
   every tool call — tool name, sanitized params, client ID, duration,
   success/failure. Content fields redacted to avoid PII in logs.

3. Per-tool scope enforcement (MCP-T2): Each tool declares required
   scopes (e.g. elnora_upload_file needs files:write). Insufficient
   scopes return an error before the tool executes. Least privilege.

4. Rate limiting on /mcp (MCP-T10): 60 req/min per client with
   standard X-RateLimit-* headers and Retry-After on 429.

5. SBOM generation (MCP-T11): CI pipeline generates CycloneDX SBOM
   on every build, uploaded as artifact for supply chain visibility.

Also: payload size limit (1MB) on express.json() body parser.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat(ci): add staging phoenix test pipeline

Provisions a fresh VPS from scratch on every push to main, runs full
Ansible provisioning (2 agents, MCP stack, Telegram, Obsidian, qmd,
sandbox), verifies deployment, smoke-tests model connectivity, then
destroys everything. Cost: ~€0.01/run.

- staging.yml: full phoenix test (provision → verify → smoke → destroy)
- staging-cleanup.yml: daily orphan cleanup for failed runs
- smoke-test.sh: device pairing, health check, doctor validation
- firewall.ts: accept optional hetznerName to avoid name collisions
- index.ts: pass server-scoped firewall name, add obsidian secrets
  to provisionEnv (fixes gap: obsidian-headless via pulumi up)

commit.message:
Strengthen manuscript for eLife: reframe as universal scaling law paper (#1144)

Major restructuring to address Nature/Nat Comms rejection feedback that the
work lacked "a sufficiently strong or broadly impactful advance."

Narrative reframing:
- New title: "The Allometric Trap: Scaling Laws Predict When Growing
  Organisms Cannot Afford Their Own Shape"
- Lead with universal scaling law (Bio-Gravitational Number), not scoliosis
- New abstract (~170 words) emphasizing the scaling law discovery
- Impact statement for eLife submission
- Updated Model Summary box with Allometric Trap framing

Content reorganization:
- Results reordered: cross-species validation first, then energy deficit,
  protein landscape, S-curve emergence, phase diagram, scoliosis bifurcation
- Added prominent predictions table (6 falsifiable predictions with
  quantitative thresholds)
- Removed duplicate Energy Deficit Window paragraphs
- Added cross-species Bg table (12 species with literature citations)

Theory section overhaul:
- Condensed from ~227 lines to ~100 lines (key equations only)
- Created Supplementary Information with full Cosserat derivations,
  biological metric, Shannon/KL formalism, protein dynamics, spinal clock
- Removed GR/spacetime analogies, Active Inference framing from main text

Data updates:
- Updated all protein anisotropy values to AlphaFold v6 (72% gap, p=0.011)
- Added PTK7, DSTYK, PPARGC1A, ARNTL, ACAN, MMP1/3 to protein table
- Updated species_parameters.csv with literature citations and 2 new species
- Added missing references (niklas1994plant, oconnell2007scaling)

Discussion/Conclusion:
- Condensed to 4 focused subsections
- Added Allometric Trap as universal constraint section
- Connected to West-Brown-Enquist allometric scaling and tree height limits
- Clear limitations section

New cover letter targeting eLife with scaling law emphasis.

https://claude.ai/code/session_015NsD6hk3zuPHJNscEmgsVA

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix iOS paste menu and skip error

iOS paste menu fix:
- Input opacity must be 1 (not 0.01) — iOS ignores long-press paste on
  near-invisible elements. Use color:transparent + background:transparent
  instead so the input is technically visible but looks invisible.
- Add -webkit-user-select:text so iOS selection/paste is allowed.
- Keep caretColor at rgba(255,255,255,0.5) so iOS has an anchor point
  for the paste menu position.

Skip error fix:
- When user taps SKIP and the save fails (network/Supabase error),
  proceed to the leaderboard anyway instead of showing ERROR — RETRY.
  The save is optional when skipping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement SageAgent ADK based on OpenSage whitepaper

Full implementation of the five core capabilities from arxiv.org/abs/2602.16891:
- Agent Engine: lifecycle management, iterative LLM+tool execution loop
- Topology Manager: LLM-driven task decomposition into DAGs, sub-agent spawning
- Toolset Generator: dynamic runtime tool creation, built-in tools (shell, file ops, code analysis, Docker)
- Memory Graph: NetworkX DAG with typed nodes, garbage collection, query interface
- Agent Communication: async pub/sub message bus with typed protocols

Also includes:
- Pluggable LLM backends (Claude, OpenAI) with common async interface
- Typer CLI with run and config commands
- OpenSpec specs, design docs, and architecture diagrams (paperbanana)
- Pre-commit hooks enforcing black, ruff, gitleaks, and 100% test coverage
- 217 tests with 100% line and branch coverage
- Comprehensive README with usage examples and tutorials

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add check-front-matter, check-capability-scope, and shared parsing lib

New tools for the PR review CI pipeline:
- tools/lib/parse-front-matter.js: shared parsing for all artifact types
  (markdown YAML, Gherkin comments, JSON _meta, YAML top-level fields)
- tools/check-front-matter.js: validates id/type/refs on spec files
- tools/check-capability-scope.js: validates changed specs appear in a
  capability scope block and scope entries point to existing files
- tools/check-traceability.js: rewritten to prefer front-matter refs
  with fallback to legacy conventions; now checks personas, journeys,
  stories, features, fixtures, journey maps, and models; adds --json flag

All three scripts support --json output for GitHub Action parsing and
--files for PR-diff-scoped checking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add direct innertube fallback for browsers without MAIN world support

Yandex Browser mobile doesn't support world: 'MAIN' content scripts,
so the bridge never injects and transcript loading fails. Now falls
back to direct innertube API and timedtext URL fetch from the content
script when the bridge is unavailable.

- Track bridge availability; skip after first timeout (5s)
- fetchPlayerData: bridge → direct WEB client innertube API
- fetchTranscriptViaBridge: bridge → direct timedtext URL fetch

- CSV export includes all new fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: group merchants by email only, not by merchant_id

Remove merchant_key from GROUP BY clause to properly group merchants
by normalized email address only. This prevents merchants from appearing
as duplicate rows when merchant_id differs between outreach and inbound emails.

Before: GROUP BY merchant_key, merchant_email_normalized, cohort...
  - Outreach has merchant_id = "003UW..."
  - Inbound has merchant_id = NULL or different
  - Result: 2 separate rows for same merchant

After: GROUP BY merchant_email_normalized, cohort...
  - Groups all emails for same merchant together
  - Uses MAX(merchant_key) to select one merchant_id
  - Result: 1 row per merchant

This fixes the issue where 20 merchants appeared as 22 rows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: integrate build validation tests into Docker pipeline

Add CPU-only test_build.py that runs inside the built image before ECR
push — validates imports, versions, file presence, and Vulkan ICD. If
tests fail, the build aborts and does not push to ECR. Results are
uploaded to S3 for review.

Also add cloud/ecs/test.sh + cmd_test() in run.sh for on-demand GPU
test execution via ECS (runs test_environment.py + test_functional.py).

commit.message:
fix: refresh auth token for invites, show auth errors (#99)

* fix: eliminate all visual jank sources in GTM Kanban board

- Remove transition: 'all .15s' from task cards (caused unexpected
  visual effects on any CSS property change)
- Replace transition: 'all .2s' with targeted transitions on filter
  buttons and user view tabs
- Replace direct DOM style mutations in drag handlers with React
  state (dragOverCol) for proper reconciliation
- Clean up onDragEnd to reset both dragId and dragOverCol
- Add useCallback import for future optimization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove autosave debounce, drag-and-drop, and card hover jank from GTM board

- Replace scheduleSave (600ms debounce timer) with immediate saveBoard
- Remove all drag-and-drop (dragId, dragOverCol, onDragStart, onDragEnd, onDragOver, onDragLeave, onDrop, draggable)
- Remove className="card" from MRR progress bar to eliminate CSS hover transitions
- Tasks now moved only via arrow buttons, saves happen instantly on action

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: wrap long notes/titles in GTM task cards with word-break

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: constrain GTM grid columns so long text wraps instead of overflowing

Add minWidth: 0 and overflow: hidden to column containers so CSS grid
columns respect 1fr width instead of expanding to fit content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: allow super admins to invite team members

- Frontend: treat app_role='admin' as team owner in TeamsPage so they
  can see the Settings tab and use the invite UI
- Backend: invite-member edge function now checks caller's app_role
  and allows admins to bypass the team.created_by ownership check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: check team owner's plan (not caller's) when admin sends invite

The plan/billing check used userId (the caller) to look up the plan,
but when a super admin sends the invite, their profile has no paid plan.
Now uses team.created_by to check the actual team owner's plan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show actual error message in invite-member for debugging

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show actual error on invite failure instead of generic message

Catch network/CORS errors separately, handle non-JSON responses,
and show HTTP status code when error body is empty.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: disable gateway JWT verification for invite-member edge function

The Supabase gateway was rejecting requests with 401 before our
function code ran, returning non-JSON without CORS headers. Our
function already handles JWT verification internally (lines 40-57),
so gateway verification is redundant and prevents proper error responses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: super admins bypass plan/billing and member limit checks for invites

Super admins (app_role='admin') now skip all plan, billing, and member
count checks when sending invites. Previously failed because admin
plan is not in PLAN_MAX_MEMBERS map.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add Dashboard button to desktop nav bar before Teams

  (was reading from stale AuthContext profile instead of updated members state)
- Hide invite code from non-admin/non-owner team members
- Allow all members to view group dashboard (roster + challenges only)
- Hide listings and standups from regular members in group dashboard

fix: single-instance probe-connect before removeServer() prevents dual launch

Previously removeServer() blindly deleted the socket of a running instance,
allowing a second instance to start. Now a probe-connect checks liveness first.
Also: IPC send_message logging, waitForDisconnected, socket deleteLater.

- Waitlist table migration (farmer_waitlist with FIFO, token index)
- waitlist_service.py: add_to_waitlist, notify_next_waitlisted (FIFO email), validate/mark token
- Translations: 4 waitlist keys x 7 languages (en, sl, hr, sr, bg, ro, it)

Remove pterm from Bazel build files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: corrigir contagem de alta prioridade nos apontamentos Construflow

API do Construflow retorna prioridade como valor numérico ('3' = alta),
mas o frontend só verificava strings 'high' e 'alta'. Adicionado suporte
a valores numéricos no PRIORITY_TRANSLATION e criado helper isHighPriority
para centralizar a lógica (mesmo padrão do weekly-report-generator).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct email extraction using SUBSTRING instead of REGEXP_REPLACE

Replace REGEXP_REPLACE with SUBSTRING for email normalization to fix
issue where inbound emails were returning control characters instead
of actual email addresses.

Before: REGEXP_REPLACE returning '\u0001' for "Name <email>" format
After: SUBSTRING FROM '<([^>]+)>' properly extracts email address

This fixes TruGlow appearing as 2 rows (one with blank email) and
ensures all merchants group correctly by normalized email.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
